word: G63 - ἀγραυλέω - agrauléō - ag-row-leh'-o - Abide in the field

sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἀγραυλέω

- Transliteration: Agrauleo

- Phonetic: ag-row-leh'-o

- Definition:

1. to live in the fields, be under the open sky, even at night

- Origin: from G68 and G832 (in the sense of G833)

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G68 and G832 (in the sense of G833); to camp out: - abide in the field.
